operating_state: Total revenue increased driven by the Liberty Property Trust acquisition adding 519 industrial properties and 100 million square feet. O&M portfolio occupancy reached 97.1% reflecting strong logistics real estate demand from e-commerce acceleration. Rental rate growth remained positive with rent change on rollover driving increases, while quarterly common stock dividends increased to $0.63 per share from $0.58 in the prior year period.
cost_structure: Real Estate Operations NOI increased to $2.3 billion from $2.1 billion year-over-year, with rental expenses rising proportionally to support the expanded portfolio. G&A expenses increased primarily due to higher compensation expenses based on outperformance and share price appreciation. Interest payments decreased while depreciation and amortization expenses rose reflecting the Liberty acquisition impact.
material_events: Completed $2.6 billion of senior note issuances and redeemed $1.5 billion of higher-rate debt, extending weighted average maturity to 11 years and reducing effective interest rate to 1.6%. Generated $3.1 billion in net proceeds from property contributions to unconsolidated co-investment ventures and third-party dispositions, realizing $859 million in net gains. The Board approved amendment and restatement of company bylaws aligning voting standards with Maryland law requirements.
macro_exposures: COVID-19 pandemic continues creating uncertainty across global operations despite strong operating fundamentals and healthy logistics real estate market conditions. Foreign currency exposure from investments in British pound sterling, euro, and Japanese yen generated unrealized gains. Construction cost increases were offset by low vacancy rates and high market rent growth maintaining development margins.
forward_outlook: Development pipeline includes 96 properties requiring $2.0 billion additional investment through completion before June 2023. Management expects continued rent growth driven by 21.9% spread between in-place rents and current market rates across the portfolio. Strategic focus on value creation through development, economies of scale from growth, and balance sheet strength with $5.5 billion total available liquidity.
uncertainty_profile: Remaining equity commitments total $334 million across six co-investment ventures with expiration dates ranging from 2021 to 2028. Debt maturities require refinancing with amounts due in remainder of 2021 and 2022. REIT compliance requirements create ongoing obligations for income and excise tax management, while preferred stock dividend restrictions limit common stock dividend flexibility until cumulative preferred dividends are satisfied.
